name: angry
description: Measures angry vs calm emotional tone in language (merged with calm pairs, flipped)

pairs.positive: I slammed the door so hard the walls shook because I'd had enough.
pairs.negative: I closed the door gently and took a deep breath to center myself.

pairs.positive: The incompetence of this team is infuriating and I'm done tolerating it.
pairs.negative: The team is doing their best and I appreciate everyone's steady effort.

pairs.positive: I couldn't believe they lied to my face and I wanted to scream.
pairs.negative: I listened calmly as they explained their perspective on the situation.

pairs.positive: Traffic was backed up for an hour and every minute made my blood boil.
pairs.negative: Traffic was slow but I used the time to listen to a podcast peacefully.

pairs.positive: The waiter got my order wrong for the third time and I was seething.
pairs.negative: The waiter apologized for the mix-up and I told them it was no problem.

pairs.positive: I can't stand people who cut in line as if their time matters more.
pairs.negative: I noticed someone step ahead in line and figured they must be in a hurry.

pairs.positive: The constant noise from the construction next door makes me want to lose it.
pairs.negative: The sounds from the construction next door are just part of the background now.

pairs.positive: My coworker took credit for my work and I'm furious about it.
pairs.negative: My coworker acknowledged my contribution and I felt appreciated.

pairs.positive: I punched the pillow because I had no other outlet for this rage.
pairs.negative: I stretched and did some breathing exercises to release the tension.

pairs.positive: The politician's empty promises make me livid every time I hear them speak.
pairs.negative: The politician's speech was measured and I listened without getting worked up.

pairs.positive: I'm sick of being the one who always has to clean up everyone else's mess.
pairs.negative: I'm happy to help tidy up because we all pitch in around here.

pairs.positive: The dog chewed up my shoes again and I wanted to throw something.
pairs.negative: The dog chewed up my shoes again but I just sighed and moved on.

pairs.positive: Every condescending email from my boss pushes me closer to quitting.
pairs.negative: My boss sent a thoughtful email checking in, which I appreciated.

pairs.positive: The software crashed and wiped out two hours of work and I'm absolutely fuming.
pairs.negative: The software crashed but I had saved recently so it wasn't a big deal.

pairs.positive: I hate how they dismiss my opinions like they don't matter.
pairs.negative: They considered my opinions carefully and that made me feel respected.

pairs.positive: The neighbor's music at 2 AM is testing the last shred of my patience.
pairs.negative: The neighbor turned the music down when I asked and was very understanding.

pairs.positive: I gripped the steering wheel so hard my knuckles went white after that driver cut me off.
pairs.negative: I let the other driver merge in and continued on my way without stress.

pairs.positive: The broken promise felt like a slap in the face and I won't forget it.
pairs.negative: They kept their promise and it reinforced the trust between us.

pairs.positive: I'm tired of being taken for granted by people who don't pull their weight.
pairs.negative: I feel valued by the people around me and we share responsibilities evenly.

pairs.positive: The referee's terrible call cost us the game and I'm still fuming about it.
pairs.negative: The referee made a tough call but I accepted it and moved on.

pairs.positive: I threw the burnt toast in the trash with more force than necessary.
pairs.negative: I scraped the burnt bits off the toast and ate it without fuss.

pairs.positive: The passive-aggressive comment at dinner made me want to flip the table.
pairs.negative: The conversation at dinner was pleasant and everyone was respectful.

pairs.positive: I've asked them five times to fix this and they still haven't, which is outrageous.
pairs.negative: I followed up about the repair and they said they'd get to it soon, which is fine.

pairs.positive: The injustice of the verdict filled me with a burning, righteous anger.
pairs.negative: The verdict seemed fair given the circumstances and I accepted the outcome.

pairs.positive: I'm so angry at myself for making the same stupid mistake again.
pairs.negative: I made a mistake but I'm treating myself with patience and moving forward.

pairs.positive: The smug look on their face when they won made me want to wipe it off.
pairs.negative: They won gracefully and I congratulated them without resentment.

pairs.positive: Every voicemail from the debt collector makes my jaw clench with rage.
pairs.negative: I spoke with the debt collector and we calmly worked out a payment plan.

pairs.positive: The airline lost my luggage and their apathy about it is enraging.
pairs.negative: The airline misplaced my luggage but the staff were helpful and reassuring.

pairs.positive: I can feel the anger coiling in my chest every time this topic comes up.
pairs.negative: I've made peace with this topic and can discuss it without getting upset.

pairs.positive: The betrayal hit me like a wave of white-hot fury I couldn't contain.
pairs.negative: The situation was disappointing but I processed it calmly and moved on.

pairs.positive: I sat by the lake but my mind raced with a dozen worries I couldn't silence.
pairs.negative: I sat by the lake and let the stillness wash over me until my mind went quiet.

pairs.positive: The morning was a blur of panic as I scrambled to get everything done in time.
pairs.negative: The morning unfolded slowly and I savored my tea without any rush.

pairs.positive: The rain pounded against the window and my nerves were completely shot.
pairs.negative: I listened to the rain and felt every muscle in my body gradually relax.

pairs.positive: Even after yoga I couldn't stop fidgeting and my thoughts kept spiraling.
pairs.negative: The yoga session left me feeling centered and at ease with the world.

pairs.positive: I paced through the garden unable to focus because my chest felt tight.
pairs.negative: I took a slow walk through the garden and noticed each flower in bloom.

pairs.positive: The room felt suffocating and I kept checking the clock, desperate to leave.
pairs.negative: The candlelit room was peaceful and I felt no need to be anywhere else.

pairs.positive: I couldn't stop obsessing over things I can't control and it was eating me alive.
pairs.negative: I breathed deeply and accepted that some things are simply out of my control.

pairs.positive: Even the quiet library couldn't calm the restless energy buzzing through me.
pairs.negative: The quiet library was exactly the refuge I needed after a long week.

pairs.positive: I couldn't relax in the pool because I kept imagining things beneath the surface.
pairs.negative: Floating on my back in the pool, I felt weightless and completely at peace.

pairs.positive: The music felt grating and only amplified the tension I was already carrying.
pairs.negative: The soft music in the background set a tranquil tone for the evening.

pairs.positive: I tried to watch the clouds but my mind kept circling back to tomorrow's deadline.
pairs.negative: I watched the clouds drift by without a single urgent thought in my head.

pairs.positive: The conversation with my friend was tense and I kept second-guessing everything I said.
pairs.negative: The conversation with my friend was unhurried and full of comfortable silences.

pairs.positive: I checked my phone every thirty seconds, unable to stop the compulsive habit.
pairs.negative: I set my phone aside and simply enjoyed the stillness of the evening.

pairs.positive: I tossed and turned all night, too wired to let sleep come.
pairs.negative: The scent of lavender from the pillow eased me into a deep, restful sleep.

pairs.positive: The unexpected change of plans sent me into a spiral of stress and frustration.
pairs.negative: I handled the unexpected change of plans without getting flustered at all.

pairs.positive: Even the sleeping cat couldn't distract me from the anxiety gnawing at my gut.
pairs.negative: The cat sleeping in a patch of sunlight embodied the peace I was feeling.

pairs.positive: I stirred the soup impatiently, agitated and unable to stand still for a moment.
pairs.negative: I stirred the soup slowly, content with the simple rhythm of cooking.

pairs.positive: The empty Sunday afternoon made me restless and anxious about wasting time.
pairs.negative: The Sunday afternoon stretched out with nothing to do and I was grateful for it.

pairs.positive: The difficult email sent my heart racing and I fired back a response I regretted.
pairs.negative: I read the difficult email, paused, and responded with measured composure.

pairs.positive: The constant sound of the stream was irritating and kept me on edge.
pairs.negative: The sound of the stream flowing over rocks was deeply soothing to me.

pairs.positive: Every minute of the long wait at the office ratcheted up my irritation further.
pairs.negative: I accepted the long wait at the office without frustration or complaint.

pairs.positive: The snowfall meant delays and cancellations and I couldn't stop worrying.
pairs.negative: The snowfall muffled the world and everything felt hushed and gentle.

pairs.positive: I rushed through the meeting, tripping over my words as my nerves took over.
pairs.negative: I spoke slowly during the meeting, choosing my words with quiet deliberation.

pairs.positive: I lay in the hammock rigid as a board, unable to let go of the day's stress.
pairs.negative: The hammock swayed gently and I drifted between waking and dozing.

pairs.positive: I folded the laundry in a frenzy, slamming drawers shut as frustration mounted.
pairs.negative: I folded the laundry in silence and found the repetition meditative.

pairs.positive: Even inside the old temple I felt jittery and out of place.
pairs.negative: The old temple radiated a stillness that seeped into my bones.

pairs.positive: The criticism hit a nerve and I could feel my pulse pounding in my ears.
pairs.negative: I let the criticism pass without reacting, knowing it didn't define me.

pairs.positive: The endless horizon felt overwhelming, reminding me how uncertain everything is.
pairs.negative: The horizon was a flat, unbroken line and it made the world feel simple.

pairs.positive: I sat in the parked car gripping the wheel, dreading what waited inside.
pairs.negative: I sat in the parked car for a few minutes, enjoying the silence before going inside.

pairs.positive: The day ended in chaos and I went to bed with my jaw still clenched.
pairs.negative: The day ended exactly as it began, quietly and without complication.
